title: Bitter Velvety British Chicken Stew
description: A low-calorie, solo-dinner British dish featuring cranberry and steam, with a bitter and velvety texture. This stew is perfect for a cold winter evening.
instructions: Heat the bitter ale in a large pot over medium heat until it starts to simmer.
Add the chopped onion, carrots, and celery, and cook until the vegetables are tender, about 10 minutes.
Add the chicken breast and cook until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the chicken broth, cranberry sauce, arrowroot powder, thyme, and black pepper. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20-25 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the sauce has thickened.
Remove the pot from the heat and let it steam, covered, for 5 minutes.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with fresh thyme if desired.
